stepTitle: C) What is the probability of getting exactly $$1$$ head or exactly $$2$$ heads?
stepBody: For parts (B) - (D), assume we have a biased coin such that the probability of getting heads is $$\frac{1}{5}$$ and the probability of getting tails is $$\frac{4}{5}$$. The coin is tossed $$3$$ times.
